title: Becium modestum
taxonomy: Becium modestum is a species of plant that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. This family includes a wide variety of plants commonly known as mint, basil, and oregano. Becium modestum is a flowering plant, and its classification is as follows: Kingdom - Plantae, Clade - Angiosperms, Clade - Eudicots, Clade - Asterids, Order - Lamiales, Family - Lamiaceae, Genus - Becium, Species - B. modestum. The classification of Becium modestum highlights its place among other flowering plants in the Lamiaceae family. This classification provides valuable information about the plant's evolutionary history and relationships with other species.
uses: Becium modestum, a plant species native to parts of Africa, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits.
